session_hint: the cron job double-execution and duplicate invoice investigation
question: What did we conclude was causing the cron job to execute twice and generate duplicate invoices?
turns: user

Our nightly invoice generation cron is producing duplicate invoices. Two identical invoice records per customer, 12 hours apart... wait, no — same timestamp. Both running at exactly 02:00 UTC. Started when we scaled from one to two app servers last sprint.
user
Checked timezone configuration on both servers: both are set to UTC, confirmed via timedatectl. The cron expression '0 2 * * *' is in UTC. No timezone mismatch.
user
Reviewed the cron expression syntax carefully: '0 2 * * *' — fires at minute 0, hour 2, every day. Syntactically correct.
user
Considered job queue depth: we don't use a job queue for this cron. It's a plain crontab entry that shells out to a Python script. No queue involved.
user

Root cause: the cron job is defined in /etc/cron.d/invoice-gen and both app servers have identical crontabs installed via our Ansible playbook. At 02:00 both servers fire the job simultaneously with no coordination mechanism — no distributed lock, no leader election, no database semaphore. Two processes run the same invoice script at the same time on different servers.
user
Confirmed by checking timestamps in the invoice table: duplicate pairs have creation timestamps within 200ms of each other, consistent with two servers starting simultaneously, not a retry or re-run.
user
Two fix approaches: (1) add a Redis-based distributed lock using SETNX with a TTL, (2) designate one server as the cron host and remove crontab from the others.
user

Went with the dedicated cron server approach — simpler and removes the risk of the lock failing. Removed crontab from both app servers and added it to the ops-jobs server that already exists for batch work.
user
Timezone config and cron expression syntax were both correct all along. Job queue depth is irrelevant since there is no queue.
user
Added a database-level unique constraint on (billing_period, customer_id) in the invoices table as a safety net against any future double-execution.
user
Cleaned up the duplicate invoices with a migration script. Verified no billing impact — payment processing had already deduped on the Stripe side. Incident closed.
